goal: Create shared source and provenance presentation primitives so Orbit can show readable source context first and keep raw evidence identifiers inside optional disclosures.

success_criteria.description: shared/ui/source-chip.tsx exports SourceChip with typed props for human-readable source label, source type, trust state, and optional supporting text; rendered visible text never includes raw evidence identifiers, queue identifiers, or source identifiers.

shared/ui/provenance-disclosure.tsx exports ProvenanceDisclosure with typed props for summary label, source label, evidence identifiers, and optional notes; raw identifiers render only inside a details disclosure body using code/list markup, not in the summary or primary label.

SourceChip and ProvenanceDisclosure compose with existing shared primitives and CSS class names, use no inline style attributes, provide accessible labels or summaries, and keep text wrapping stable for long labels.

tests/ui/source-provenance.test.tsx renders success, empty, long-label, and raw-identifier cases, asserts primary text excludes raw IDs, asserts details content contains raw IDs when supplied, and asserts unknown trust states fall back to a neutral presentation.
No feature contracts, service factories, route handlers, route pages, API envelopes, or sample data fixtures are changed; npm test and npm run build pass.

out_of_scope: Retrofitting every product route to use the new source and provenance components
Changing API envelopes or route handlers
Changing sample data fixtures or service factories
Changing public route copy from Sprint 71
